response.justification: Strong individual-contributor background with 13+ years of polyglot software development, but the role requires a Level II Engineering Manager with explicit people-management experience, developer-tools/build-systems focus, and mobile-platform exposure. The resume shows no formal people-management tenure, no compiler/build-system/test-infrastructure leadership, and only incidental mobile work (iOS/Apple TV maintenance ~2015-2016). Technical leadership signals exist (Staff titles, workshops, OSS founder), but they don't substitute for the required 2 years managing people or 3 years building dev tools at the depth this role demands.
strengths: 13+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded
Developer-tooling adjacency: MCP code generators, CI/CD setup, test runner (Trial), assertion library (Fluent-Asserts)
Demonstrated knowledge sharing via workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: No documented people-management or direct-report leadership experience
Mobile-platform experience is thin and dated (Apple TV/iOS ~2015-2016), not at ecosystem scale
No evidence of leading organizational change or owning a team roadmap

key_factors.factor: Long, varied IC engineering track record
